toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try Microsoft symbol server - debuginfod protocol
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try alternative URL format
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try the executable itself (not debug info)
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try dotnet symbol server / NuGet debuginfod
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbolserver.unity3d.com/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>&1
echo ""
arguments.description: Try downloading debug symbols from symbol servers

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try downloading debug symbols from symbol servers

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key information is:

1. The crash is in `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (the Mono runtime used by .NET MAUI on Android)
2. The BuildId is `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`
3. We have PC offsets for frames #01 through #07

To symbolicate these, I would need:
- The debug symbols for this specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (matching the BuildId)


- A tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses

I should try fetching the symbols from Microsoft's symbol server using the BuildId, since they publish debug symbols for .NET runtime libraries.

The Microsoft symbol server uses a specific URL format for ELF files with BuildId. I can try constructing the URL with the BuildId embedded in the path, or use the debuginfod protocol as an alternative approach.

Let me check what debugging tools are available on the system and attempt to fetch the symbols using one of these methods. Let me try different URL patterns to locate the symbols, and check what debugging tools I have available on this system.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line readelf objdump 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which dotnet 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

echo ""
# Try alternative URL format
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try the executable itself (not debug info)
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try dotnet symbol server / NuGet debuginfod
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbolserver.unity3d.com/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>&1
echo ""

toolArgs.command:
# Try Microsoft symbol server - debuginfod protocol
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try alternative URL format
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try the executable itself (not debug info)
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try dotnet symbol server / NuGet debuginfod
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbolserver.unity3d.com/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>&1
echo ""
toolArgs.description: Try downloading debug symbols from symbol servers
